---
title: "Edward Hoffmann"
pagetitle: "Edward Hoffmann"
toc: false
format:
  html:
    title-block-style: none
---

::: {.hero .hero-with-photo}

::: {.hero-photo}

![](./images/profile.png){alt="Edward Hoffmann"}

:::

::: {.hero-copy}

Economics and statistics (UC Davis, '25). Currently working as a research and teaching assistant at UC Berkeley and as a quantitative researcher for Helix Consulting.

:::

:::

## About {#about}

I'm passionate about the theoretical foundations of decision making and learning. My academic background includes graduate coursework in optimization, game theory, and classical ML.

Fun fact: I used to play a lot of Magic: The Gathering and was once interviewed by MTGGoldfish.

## Interests {#interests}

### Research & technical

- Decision making under uncertainty: algorithmic game theory and reinforcement learning.
- Deep learning dynamics: structural and geometric properties of learning in neural networks.
- System dynamics & control: ergodicity, optimal control, and resource allocation.

### Hobbies

- Exercise
- Reading history and philosophy
- Cooking, gardening, and sewing
- Strategy games

## Projects {#projects}

### [A Bird’s-Eye View: Hawkes Processes in Texting Data](posts/hawkes-text-messages.qmd)

Fitting and comparing Hawkes process models to text message data.

## Upcoming {#upcoming}
### Functional Dimension as a motivation for MUON
Explore possible connections between functional dimension and MUON for ReLU neural networks. Will be exploring this with toy models similar to the work by Dr. Grigsby.
    
    - [Functional Dimension](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2209.04036)
    - [MUON](https://kellerjordan.github.io/posts/muon/)

### Learned geometry of “thinking” in transformers

Exploring how depth and residual structure shape internal representations during training—connecting ideas from [Florentin Guth](https://florentinguth.github.io/publication/rainbow/) on layer-wise dynamics with [David Noel Ng](https://dnhkng.github.io/posts/rys-ii/) on geometry and optimization in deep networks. Hoping I can investigate this with local compute.

### Random future ideas, potential projects (and notes for me)

- Mess around with various RL architectures and use-cases
- MtG RL (the dream)
- Visual math exploration à la 3b1b about differential geometry, geodesics, and gradient descent
    - [Differential geometry library](https://geomstats.github.io/index.html)
- Jane Street puzzle solutions
- Some geospatial analysis (pretty but I already do this stuff at work so not currently something I want to work on in my free time so we'll see)
- Playing with signal processing/error correction methods
- Explore/play with an ergodic system
    - [Ergodic RL](https://ergodicityeconomics.com/2023/10/20/an-ergodicity-perspective-on-reinforcement-learning/)
- PINNs / energy-based models
    - [Energy based models](https://energy-based-model.github.io/Energy-based-Model-MIT/)
    - [Steve Brunton](https://www.youtube.com/@Eigensteve/playlists?theme=dark)
    - [Ben Moseley](https://benmoseley.blog/)
- Weather forecasting model
